title: SyncSender(256) can block async task on event burst
description: main.rs:23: task_tx is a std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender<AppEvent> with capacity 256. send_event() (client_task.rs:580) calls tx.send() which is a blocking call made directly from the async background task (not from spawn_blocking). If the UI falls behind and 256 events queue up, the 257th send() will park the calling thread, stalling the entire tokio runtime on that thread. At normal usage this does not trigger, but during a burst (e.g. initial load of 200 chats + messages + status events) it could. Fix: use tokio::sync::mpsc or increase the bound; alternatively batch multiple events into a single AppEvent::Batch variant to reduce channel pressure.
close_reason: Stale: AppEvent channel was switched to tokio unbounded_channel; send() never blocks
